Renfield (2023)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $17,297,895Details
International Box Office $9,576,452
Worldwide Box Office $26,874,347
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $705,466
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,064,170
Total $1,769,636
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Renfield is the tortured aide to history’s most narcissistic boss, Dracula. Renfield is forced to procure his master’s prey and do his every bidding, no matter how debased. But now, after centuries of servitude, Renfield is ready to see if there’s a life outside the shadow of The Prince of Darkness. If only he can figure out how to end his codependency.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$8,026,475 (46.4% of total gross)
Legs:2.16 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:64.4% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$86,203,077 (worldwide box office is 0.3 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,375 opening theaters/3,378 max. theaters, 2.7 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $18,910,674

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: April 14th, 2023 (Wide) by Universal
International Releases: April 6th, 2023 (Wide), released as Renfield (Ренфилд) (Russia (CIS))
April 7th, 2023 (Wide) (South Africa)
April 12th, 2023 (Wide) (Sweden)
April 13th, 2023 (Wide) (Paraguay)
April 13th, 2023 (Wide) (Venezuela)
Video Release: May 2nd, 2023 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for bloody violence, some gore, language throughout and some drug use.
(Rating bulletin 2763 (cert #54201), 12/21/2022)
Running Time: 93 minutes
Filming location (city or town): New Orleans, LA
Filming location (country): United States
Filming location (state or county): Louisiana, United States
Plot point: Boss From Hell, Vampire
Social setting: Supernatural
Subgenre: Horror Comedy
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Black Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Skybound Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Renfield is the tortured aide to history’s most narcissistic boss, Dracula. Renfield is forced to procure his master’s prey and do his every bidding, no matter how debased.
